title: Audit RAK4631 bridge USB CDC build warning
description: During project-LICHEN-2067.1.1.1 no-hardware EC2 validation, rak4631/nrf52840 bridge-zephyr target auto-merged firmware/bridge-zephyr/boards/rak4631_nrf52840.conf and built successfully, but Zephyr emitted a compile warning from subsys/usb/device/class/cdc_acm.c: #warning "USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL forced to LOG_LEVEL_NONE". Decide whether the RAK4631 bridge auto-merge fragment should keep USB CDC console enabled, set/log the expected CDC logging behavior explicitly, or split LoRa-only build coverage from USB local-client behavior. No hardware required for the build-warning audit; physical USB behavior remains a separate validation concern.
notes:
Implementation decision:
- Zephyr's legacy CDC ACM driver intentionally emits #warning "USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL forced to LOG_LEVEL_NONE" when DT_CHOSEN(zephyr_console) is a zephyr,cdc-acm-uart, CONFIG_LOG_BACKEND_UART is enabled, and CONFIG_USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL is not LOG_LEVEL_NONE. This prevents a recursive logging loop through the same CDC ACM console.
- RAK4631 bridge should keep USB CDC console enabled for the local-client/console path; this audit does not split LoRa-only and USB behavior.
- Patched firmware/bridge-zephyr/boards/rak4631_nrf52840.conf to set CONFIG_USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL=0 explicitly with a comment, so the expected behavior is configured rather than forced by the driver warning.

Code review finding and fix:
- Review agents 019f2439-b2ff-7820-8945-1f472ed1ebda and 019f2439-c699-7ce2-9a38-95ebe04f00f7 both found P1: CONFIG_USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL=0 is numerically LOG_LEVEL_NONE but is not directly user-configurable in Zephyr v3.7 because USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL is a hidden derived int from logging Kconfig choice symbols.
- Fixed before commit by replacing CONFIG_USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL=0 with CONFIG_USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL_OFF=y, the user-facing choice symbol that derives LOG_LEVEL_NONE without breaking Kconfig parsing.
- Both reviewers agreed this preserves CDC ACM console behavior; EC2 RAK4631 build remains required to verify warning suppression.
